subject: Project Management
title: Scrum at Spotify
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Scrum at Spotify.

hook.text: Spotify started with textbook Scrum, then evolved beyond it as the company scaled.

buildup.text: Small squads ran sprints and retros. As Spotify grew, they needed coordination across squads.

discovery.text: They created tribes, chapters, and guilds — a custom organizational model.

twist.text: Spotify's model was a snapshot, not a prescription. They kept changing after publishing it.

climax.text: The lesson: Scrum is a starting point. Mature teams outgrow the training wheels.

punchline.text: They started with Scrum and graduated to their own.

quiz.question: What did Spotify do with Scrum as it grew?
options: Evolved beyond it into a custom organizational model
Followed the Scrum Guide more strictly
